text.title: Use Case Modeling Examples
text.summary: Place Order use case with main, alternative, and exception flows; actor-goal mapping for an e-commerce system.

example.note: Primary Actor: Customer. Preconditions: customer is logged in; cart has at least one item.
example.code:
Main Flow: | Step | Actor Action | System Response | |------|-------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------| | 1 | Customer initiates checkout | System displays order summary with totals | | 2 | Customer confirms shipping address | System calculates shipping cost | | 3 | Customer selects payment method | System presents payment entry form | | 4 | Customer submits payment details | System validates payment with payment gateway | | 5 | Customer confirms order | System creates order, reduces inventory, | | | | emits confirmation email |
Alternative Flow AF-1: New Shipping Address At step 2, customer enters a new address. System validates format and saves address to customer profile. Return to step 2 with updated address selected.
Exception Flow EX-1: Payment Declined At step 4, payment gateway returns decline response. System displays error "Payment could not be processed" with error code. Customer may retry with same or different payment method; flow resumes at step 3.
Postconditions (success): Order record created with status Confirmed; inventory decremented for each line item; confirmation email delivered. Postconditions (failure): Cart preserved; no inventory change; no charge.
example.note: Note: postconditions are observable states, not user perceptions.
E-commerce system — actor-to-use-case index used to verify complete coverage.
example.code:
| Actor | Use Case | Type | |-----------------|------------------------|---------| | Customer | Browse Products | Primary | | Customer | Place Order | Primary | | Customer | Track Shipment | Primary | | Admin | Manage Inventory | Primary | | Payment Gateway | Process Payment | System | | Warehouse System| Reserve Inventory | System |
Relationship: Place Order <<includes>> Process Payment Rationale: every order must process payment; de-duplicates the 4-step payment validation flow that would otherwise appear in Place Order, Renew Subscription, and Top-Up Account.

antipattern.description: Feature as use case: "Display order screen" — no actor goal, no system behavior.
antipattern.reason: A use case must describe a goal an actor achieves, not a screen the system renders. Rename to "View Order Details" and add actor, flows, postconditions.

antipattern.description: Overly abstract use case: "Use the system" — no specific goal, untestable.
antipattern.reason: Abstract use cases cannot produce test cases; every use case must deliver a specific, measurable outcome for a named actor.

antipattern.description: Main flow with 20 steps — two distinct actor goals bundled in one use case.
antipattern.reason: Split at the natural goal boundary. Apply the step-count rule: any main flow exceeding 9 steps is a splitting signal.
